channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 2026 cost of Defence continues a tradition of defence-spending scrutiny | ASPI Strategist
item.description: ASPI has published The cost of Defence every year since 2002. The series has, year after year, assessed what governments said they would do against what the books showed. It’s the type of scrutiny that ...

item.title: 📍 Can the U.S. End Nigeria’s Insurgency? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Despite recent successful strikes, experts doubt the campaign’s long-term success.

item.title: 🏴 Greece’s energy realism: A bridge for transatlantic energy cooperation | Atlantic Council
item.description: Greece has the resources, geography, and geopolitical partnerships to help strengthen European energy security and advance its energy transition. The post Greece’s energy realism: A bridge for transatlantic energy cooperation appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon eyes drone testing ground in Mississippi | Defense News
item.description: U.S. Special Operations Command is looking to build a new testing ground for drones in Mississippi.

item.title: 🔐 Defense Business Brief: Defense cyber champs?; HASC mark; Navy IW | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon awards Dell $9.7 billion contract to consolidate software licenses | Breaking Defense
item.description: The five-year blanket purchase agreement consolidates Microsoft 365 licenses into a single contract vehicle.

item.title: 🏴 Inside The Effort To Build Ukraine’s Ground Robot Arsenal | The War Zone
item.description: Ukraine has a voracious demand for ground drones as they increasingly perform logistics, rescue and combat operations. The post Inside The Effort To Build Ukraine’s Ground Robot Arsenal appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 House draft of defense policy bill leaves some of Trump admin’s top priorities unfunded | Defense One
item.description: The draft bill doesn’t include reconciliation dollars.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Accuses Iran of Stalling Peace Talks | Foreign Policy
item.description: At the same time, the White House is optimistic that a deal to end the war is in reach.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Is Making NATO Stronger, Lithuania’s Foreign Minister Says | Foreign Policy
item.description: He also said NATO allies should “take their part” in the Iran war.

item.title: 🏛️ Dept. of Air Force ‘still working’ to sort space programs into acquisition portfolios: Ainsworth | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Department of the Air Force in “the coming days” will announce the finalized PAE structure along with the names of each office leader, Thomas Ainsworth, acting space acquisition chief, said.

item.title: 📍 2026 cost of Defence: the budget is a promise to the future paid for by the present | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia’s 2026–27 budget commits the federal government to daily spending around A$181.9 million on defence. That headline sits on the cover of our 2026 The cost of Defence report, released today. The number is sobering. ...

item.title: 🔐 US munitions depleted by Iran war will take years to restore, analysis finds | Defense News
item.description: Replenishing stockpiles of critical weapons systems could take until 2030 or 2031, creating a "window of vulnerability," a new analysis found.

item.title: 🔐 Iran’s hackers are coordinating more closely: Israeli cyber leader | Defense One
item.description: Yossi Karadi is seeking access to advanced models like Anthropic’s Mythos to help defend Israeli government networks.

item.title: ⚛️ What Did the NPT Review Conference Achieve? | War on the Rocks
item.description: The 11th Review Conference of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) concluded on May 22. Held every five years, the conference offers an opportunity to evaluate the treaty’s implementation, respond to technological and geopolitical developments, and reinforce states’…

item.title: 📍 Rubio’s Visit Won’t Assuage India | Foreign Policy
item.description: Repairing Trump’s damage to bilateral ties will require more than a charm offensive.

item.title: 🌏 How US Army combat medics are preparing for an Indo-Pacific fight | Defense News
item.description: Distance, contested air, communication disruptions, weather and limited evacuation platforms could greatly impact timely access to medical care.

item.title: 🏴 Airbus Looks To Sweden’s Saab As Europe’s Sixth-Gen Fighter Plans Unravel | The War Zone
item.description: As FCAS tensions deepen, Airbus is exploring alternatives with Saab, raising the prospect of a shakeup in Europe’s future air combat plans. The post Airbus Looks To Sweden’s Saab As Europe’s Sixth-Gen Fighter Plans Unravel appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Congress Moves To Block Trump Class Battleship Work Until Its Key Weapons Are Mature | The War Zone
item.description: Such a delay could end the battleship program before it really starts, and the House Armed Services Committee is also demanding answers on the new frigate. The post Congress Moves To Block Trump Class Battleship Work Until Its Key Weapons Are Mature appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🌏 China Needs North Korea on Its Side | Foreign Policy
item.description: A Xi visit to Pyongyang is likely soon, after a seven-year gap.

item.title: 🌏 US Army integrates veterinarians into human combat care | Defense News
item.description: The Army wants to better utilize veterinarians in combat medicine to prepare for the possibility of large-scale operations in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 📍 Washington Shouldn’t Fly Solo on Building Space Superiority | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2025, Nazmelis Zengin wrote, “The Fragility of U.S. Space Power in a Multipolar World,” where she argued Washington’s space superiority could be challenged if the United States doesn’t rethink its course, taking lessons from mid-tier space powers. A year later, we asked…

item.title: 🌏 F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries | The War Zone
item.description: Swapping dated F-5s for F/A-18E/Fs would give the Navy a more threat representative adversary fleet to emulate advanced enemies, like China. The post F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 A Textbook Debate: Why Russian Historians Told Kyrgyz Historians to Ditch the Term ‘Colonialism’ | The Diplomat
item.description: If legitimacy is tied to history, then exactly how that history is interpreted matters beyond conference rooms packed with historians.

item.title: 🔐 Understanding Cambodia’s Scam Economy, with Jacob Sims | The Diplomat
item.description: The scam economy is an industrialized system that merges cyber fraud with human trafficking and money laundering, all thriving under political protection.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army receives first lightweight Javelin launchers | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Army has received its first batch of new, lighter Javelin launchers, defense contractor RTX announced in a statement Tuesday.

item.title: 🌍 After Iran attacks, Turkish industry seeing increased Gulf interest in air defense platforms | Breaking Defense
item.description: Both companies expressed their readiness to transfer technology in line with mandates in Gulf states to localize defense production.

item.title: 📍 Don’t call it a G7 comeback | Atlantic Council
item.description: The G7’s equity rebound may look broad-based. In reality, it’s largely a tale of US tech dominance, driven by the “Magnificent Seven” and an extraordinary concentration of global market power. The post Don’t call it a G7 comeback appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 How the US, allies and adversaries see the future of drone wingmen | Breaking Defense
item.description: In the third video focused on manned-unmanned teaming, we trace how the concept has evolved inside the US defense industry and among other militaries across the globe.
